Schema Gates · v4.10.0

I/O

Wire trace

Details

Beginners' Reference

What the symbols mean in this schematic:

  • ═Bus / multi-bit wire, a thicker line carries multiple bits. Look for a [N:0] tag near it to see how wide the bus is.
  • ⊓Splitter / BusSlice, a wide bus comes in, only the selected bits come out as a narrower signal.
  • ⊔Convergence / BusGroup, several individual wires combine into one bus.
  • ↗Zero / sign-extend, the input bus is widened. "0-ext" fills the new bits with 0; "sign-ext" replicates the MSB.
  • XX state (undefined), wires are orange when the simulator hasn't yet evaluated them or the value is genuinely undefined.
  • ZZ state (high-Z, tri-state), purple/dashed wires mean a driver is disabled and no one is forcing the wire to 0 or 1.
  • ▷Clock edge marker, the small triangle on a DFF marks which edge the flop captures on (pos-edge by default).
  • ┄Enable line, a thinner or dashed line on a conditional gate is its enable; the gate only updates when it is high.
  • ⏚Constant 0/1, small square symbols anchored to $true or $false nets, used when a port is tied off.

Press ? to toggle this panel.
